title: French hospitals at 'tipping point' after seven days of heatwave
subhead: Emergency services saw the health consequences of the heatwave accelerate within 24 hours, between Wednesday and Thursday. The prime minister activated level 3 of the ORSAN plan, which is designed to increase hospital staffing levels.

title: Heatwave: Macron and PM Lecornu on the defensive after criticism over France's lack of preparation

How cities in Africa are adapting to intense and deadly heat
subhead: Reflective paints, green roofs, work breaks: Many African countries are seeking innovative ways to cope with increasingly extreme temperatures.

title: US Supreme Court clears way for Trump administration's anti-immigration policies
subhead: In a historic reversal of the country's tradition of welcoming newcomers, on Thursday, the justices authorized the administration's move to revoke protections that shielded 350,000 Haitians and 6,000 Syrians from deportation.

title: 250 years of American independence: Thomas Jefferson and John Adams, 'the North and South poles of the American Revolution'
subhead: 'America 250' (4/13). Fifty years to the day after the adoption of the Declaration of Independence, two of its architects died: the enigmatic Thomas Jefferson and the impulsive John Adams. This coincidence only strengthened, among the population, the sense of an unprecedented destiny promised to their nation.

title: Musée d'Orsay faces demand for restitution of Van Gogh painting
subhead: The descendant of a German Jewish art collector persecuted by the Nazis is seeking the return of the painting 'Hôpital Saint-Paul à Saint-Rémy-de-Provence.' The Commission for the Compensation of Victims of Spoliation is expected to review the case starting in September.

Serving a husband, God and America: Young female conservatives gather at San Antonio's Turning Point summit
subhead: In early June, around 3,000 women attended the Women's Leadership Summit in San Antonio, Texas. This annual event brings together a variety of conservative figures and influencers, relying on the extensive network of Turning Point USA, founded by right-wing activist Charlie Kirk, who was murdered in 2025. After convincing a majority of young men to vote for Trump in 2024, the organization now targets female voters from Gen Z.

title: Heatwave in France spells uncertainty for several EDF nuclear reactors, but RTE says the overall grid is secure
subhead: France's state-owned electric utility company has shut down one nuclear reactor and reduced the output of two others. Although the extreme heat is increasing electricity demand, France has enough capacity to meet it, according to the national power grid operator.

title: France faces unprecedented heatwave: 'We are about to experience four of the hottest days ever recorded in the country'
subhead: A prolonged heatwave, rather than a brief spike, has arrived. Unusually high temperatures are set to persist across the country until at least the end of this week, exposing more than 90% of the French population to extreme and rare heat.
